key_insight: ZO-Act speeds zeroth-order fine-tuning by optimizing only low-rank activation-derived coefficients instead of perturbing full model weights, which lowers estimator variance and makes forward-only training more practical.
engineering_relevance: The method is especially useful for memory-constrained or backpropagation-free LLM adaptation, including quantized models where weights must remain frozen.
method: Compute a small activation basis once at initialization for each linear layer.
Optimize lightweight coefficient matrices in a fixed low-rank subspace using forward-only loss evaluations.
Use the explicit coefficients with momentum optimizers like Adam while keeping low-bit weights frozen.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent improvements over strong zeroth-order baselines on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B across language understanding, QA, and commonsense reasoning tasks.
watch_item: Performance still depends on how well the activation-based low-rank subspace captures the true gradient directions, so subspace approximation bias can limit gains in some layers or tasks.
paper_title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
